events.title: Open Court - Basketball | Jumpstart Multi Sport Court
events.description:
Check out the Jumpstart Multi Sport Court at Panorama! Free, accessible, recreational play for all ages. 

Space includes 8 basketball nets (2 adjustable), a convertible net (set to high or low height dependent on the day), picnic tables and a free play space. Sun shades are hung seasonally. Lines and nets are recreational; not regulation.

Bring or borrow equipment. Equipment may be signed out at Reception (available first come, first served).

Please limit play to 30 minutes if others are waiting. 

Code of Conduct:  Users are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: Water Walking
events.description: Minimum 1 lane available for water walking in the leisure pool. Leisure pool, hot tub, sauna, and steam room available.

events.title: All Levels Pickleball | Reservable
events.description:
Join us for All Levels Reservable Pickleball at Greenglade Community Centre! All skill levels welcome. Bring your own paddle and ball (a couple paddles are available to borrow from Reception). 

Check-in with Reception upon arrival. Consecutive reservations not permitted. Continued play welcome, space permitting on the day (check with Reception at the start of the next session).

******

Family drop-in rate available! Please call or email Reception for this discounted admission type. Family rate includes 2 adults and up to 3 youth (max 18 years), or 1 adult and up to 4 youth (max 18 years), living at the same address.

******

Waitlist:  If you waitlist and a space becomes available, you will be notified by email. You will be asked to accept or decline the space. Please do so in a timely manner so those subsequently waitlisted may have opportunity to fill the space if you no longer want it. 

Refund/Cancellation:  Full refund for cancellations made with more than 24 hours' notice. Cancel your session online via your account. No refund for cancellations made with less than 24 hours' notice. No-shows will be charged.

Code of Conduct:  Players are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and respectful, both on and off the court. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, positive and respectful environment where everyone is valued. All staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to treat others and our facilities with care and respect.

events.title: Combo Fit
events.description:
Moderate intensity, moderate impact (comfort in deep water recommended). This class incorporates elements from both shallow and deep fit classes. Participants will begin in the shallow end for a warm up, then will move down to the deep end for the middle of the class, and will come back to the shallow end to finish the class. This class provides a mixture of cardio, strength, abdominal work and stretching. Use of a noodle or water belt is recommended for exercise in the deep end.
Admission: Use your Esquimalt Recreation or Regional Recreation Pass, or pay regular drop in.

events.title: Skate - Stick & Puck Reserved Drop In 19yrs+ GOALIE
events.facility_name: GRP Ice Rink Green - Lesson Area 2
events.description: Enjoy practicing your goaltending in the net during a Stick and Puck session. Full gear required. Maximum 2 goalies can register. You must reserve your spot to participate. Any goalie not registered as a goalie will be asked to remain a skater. Only one net is used for goalies, the other net will remain open. Goalies must be respectful and take turns being in net. Close the arena gates behind you. You must check in with reception and the skate attendant before going on the ice to validate your reservation. Please refer to the guidelines on our website for further details.

events.title: Skate - Stick & Puck Reserved Drop In 19yrs+ SKATER
events.facility_name: GRP Ice Rink Green - Lesson Area 1
events.description: Enjoy shooting on the net without goalies. Helmet, gloves, stick and skates mandatory. You must reserve your spot to participate. There are no slap shots, scrimmages, drills or passing down the ice. Keep the front and behind the net clear at all times. Always close the arena gates behind you. events.title: Open Court - Basketball | Jumpstart Multi Sport Court
events.description:
Check out the Jumpstart Multi Sport Court at Panorama! Free, accessible, recreational play for all ages. 

Space includes 8 basketball nets (2 adjustable), a convertible net (set to high or low height dependent on the day), picnic tables and a free play space. Sun shades are hung seasonally. Lines and nets are recreational; not regulation.

Bring or borrow equipment. Equipment may be signed out at Reception (available first come, first served).

Please limit play to 30 minutes if others are waiting. 

Code of Conduct:  Users are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: Adult 19+ Duffer Hockey - Players
events.description:

Classes can be booked up to 2 weeks in advance. Visit pass holders must pay drop-in price to book online, or call reception to book, then scan their visit pass to attend their reserved session.

events.title: Pickleball Reserved Drop-in
events.description: Pickleball is a racquet sport that combines elements of badminton, tennis, and table tennis. Players use solid paddles to hit a perforated polymer ball, similar to a wiffle ball, over a net. The sport shares features of other racquet sports, the dimensions and layout of a badminton court, and a net and rules similar to tennis, with a few modifications. Must be 16 or older, please bring your own paddle.

events.title: Volleyball Social Game Play | Reservable
events.description:
Join us for Volleyball Social Game Play! Registration required. 

Players of all skill levels who are comfortable with fundamental skills and basics of volleyball are welcome. Each session consists of three 25-minute games, allowing players opportunity to compete against different teams. All equipment provided.

Waitlist:  If you waitlist and a space becomes available, you will be notified by email. You will be asked to accept or decline the space. Please do so in a timely manner so those subsequently waitlisted may have opportunity to fill the space if you no longer want it. 

Refund/Cancellation:  Full refund for cancellations made with more than 24 hours' notice. Cancel your session online via your account. No refund for cancellations made with less than 24 hours' notice. No-shows will be charged.

Code of Conduct:  Players are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: All Levels Pickleball | Reservable
events.description:
Join us for All Levels Reservable Pickleball at Greenglade Community Centre! All skill levels welcome. Bring your own paddle and ball (a couple paddles available to borrow from Reception). 

Check-in with Reception upon arrival. Consecutive reservations not permitted. Continued play welcome, space permitting on the day (check with Reception at the start of the next session).

******

Family drop-in rate available! Please call or email Reception for this discounted admission type. Family rate includes 2 adults and up to 3 youth (max 18 years), or 1 adult and up to 4 youth (max 18 years), living at the same address.

******

Waitlist:  If you waitlist and a space becomes available, you will be notified by email. You will be asked to accept or decline the space. Please do so in a timely manner so those subsequently waitlisted may have opportunity to fill the space if you no longer want it. 

Refund/Cancellation:  Full refund for cancellations made with more than 24 hours' notice. Cancel your session online via your account. No refund for cancellations made with less than 24 hours' notice. No-shows will be charged.

Code of Conduct:  Players are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and respectful, both on and off the court. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, positive and respectful environment where everyone is valued. All staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to treat others and our facilities with care and respect.

events.title: Pickleball Reserved Drop-in
events.description:
Pickleball is a racquet sport that combines elements of badminton, tennis, and table tennis. Players use solid paddles to hit a perforated polymer ball, similar to a wiffle ball, over a net. The sport shares features of other racquet sports, the dimensions and layout of a badminton court, and a net and rules similar to tennis, with a few modifications.
Players can reserve for their desired session up to 7 days in advance.

You must check in with reception and the skate attendant before going on the ice to validate your reservation.

events.title: Open Court - Basketball | Jumpstart Multi Sport Court
events.description:
Check out the Jumpstart Multi Sport Court at Panorama! Free, accessible, recreational play for all ages. 

Space includes 8 basketball nets (2 adjustable), a convertible net (set to high or low height dependent on the day), picnic tables and a free play space. Sun shades are hung seasonally. Lines and nets are recreational; not regulation.

Bring or borrow equipment. Equipment may be signed out at Reception (available first come, first served).

Please limit play to 30 minutes if others are waiting. 

Code of Conduct:  Users are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: All Levels Pickleball | Reservable
events.description:
Join us for All Levels Reservable Pickleball at Greenglade Community Centre! All skill levels welcome. Bring your own paddle and ball (a couple paddles available to borrow from Reception). 

Check-in with Reception upon arrival. Consecutive reservations not permitted. Continued play welcome, space permitting on the day (check with Reception at the start of the next session).

******

Family drop-in rate available! Please call or email Reception for this discounted admission type. Family rate includes 2 adults and up to 3 youth (max 18 years), or 1 adult and up to 4 youth (max 18 years), living at the same address.

******

Waitlist:  If you waitlist and a space becomes available, you will be notified by email. You will be asked to accept or decline the space. Please do so in a timely manner so those subsequently waitlisted may have opportunity to fill the space if you no longer want it. 

Refund/Cancellation:  Full refund for cancellations made with more than 24 hours' notice. Cancel your session online via your account. No refund for cancellations made with less than 24 hours' notice. No-shows will be charged.

Code of Conduct:  Players are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and respectful, both on and off the court. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, positive and respectful environment where everyone is valued. All staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to treat others and our facilities with care and respect.

events.title: Pickleball Reserved Drop-in
events.description:
Pickleball is a racquet sport that combines elements of badminton, tennis, and table tennis. Players use solid paddles to hit a perforated polymer ball, similar to a wiffle ball, over a net. The sport shares features of other racquet sports, the dimensions and layout of a badminton court, and a net and rules similar to tennis, with a few modifications.
Players can reserve for their desired session up to 7 days in advance.

events.title: Open Court - Basketball | Jumpstart Multi Sport Court
events.description:
Check out the Jumpstart Multi Sport Court at Panorama! Free, accessible, recreational play for all ages. 

Space includes 8 basketball nets (2 adjustable), a convertible net (set to high or low height dependent on the day), picnic tables and a free play space. Sun shades are hung seasonally. Lines and nets are recreational; not regulation.

Bring or borrow equipment. Equipment may be signed out at Reception (available first come, first served).

Please limit play to 30 minutes if others are waiting. 

Code of Conduct:  Users are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: Open Court - Convertible Net High | Jumpstart Multi Sport Court

Space includes 8 basketball nets (2 adjustable), a convertible net (set to badminton/volleyball height or pickleball height dependent on the day), picnic tables and a free play space. Sun shades are hung seasonally. 

Bring or borrow equipment. Equipment may be signed out at Reception (available first come, first served).

Please limit play to 30 minutes if others are waiting.

Code of Conduct:  Users are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

At least 1 lane is available for lap swimming.

events.title: Combo Aquafit
events.description: All classes will provide you with a challenging workout with little to no impact on your joints! The instructor will provide different options for cardio training, strength exercise, and stretching. Includes warm-up, 30-35 minutes of cardio and strength, and stretching at the end. Class length will be 55 minutes. The hot tub, sauna and steam room are open. Change rooms and lockers are available.

events.title: Water Walking
events.description: Minimum 1 lane available for water walking in the leisure pool. Leisure pool, hot tub, sauna, and steam room available.

events.title: Deep Fit
events.description:
Moderate intensity, no impact (comfort in deep water required).
Using a belt or a noodle, this class is a great way for those with injuries or other limitations to still experience a good cardiovascular workout. This class will include strength and abdominal exercises to ensure a full body workout with no strain on bones or joints. 
Admission: Use your Esquimalt Recreation or Regional Recreation Pass, or pay regular drop in.

events.title: Intermediate (3.0-3.5) Pickleball |Reservable
events.description:
Join us for Intermediate Reservable Pickleball at Greenglade Community Centre! Intermediate, 3.0-3.5 skill levels welcome (refer to Pickleball Canada’s self-rating guide). Bring your own paddle and ball.

Check-in with Reception upon arrival. Consecutive reservations not permitted. Continued play welcome, space permitting on the day (check with Reception at the start of the next session).

******

Family drop-in rate available! Please call or email Reception for this discounted admission type. Family rate includes 2 adults and up to 3 youth (max 18 years), or 1 adult and up to 4 youth (max 18 years), living at the same address.

******

Waitlist:  If you waitlist and a space becomes available, you will be notified by email. You will be asked to accept or decline the space. Please do so in a timely manner so those subsequently waitlisted may have opportunity to fill the space if you no longer want it. 

Refund/Cancellation:  Full refund for cancellations made with more than 24 hours' notice. Cancel your session online via your account. No refund for cancellations made with less than 24 hours' notice. No-shows will be charged.

Code of Conduct:  Players are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and respectful, both on and off the court. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, positive and respectful environment where everyone is valued. All staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to treat others and our facilities with care and respect.

LIIT Workout Drop-in
events.description: Light up your fitness routine with Low Intensity Interval Training (LIIT). LIIT consists of periods of low impact exercise performed in sets of intervals combined with longer periods of rest. Enjoy the benefits of cardiovascular and strength training to challenge yourself without overtaxing the body.

events.title: Combo Fit
events.description:
Moderate intensity, moderate impact (comfort in deep water recommended). This class incorporates elements from both shallow and deep fit classes. Participants will begin in the shallow end for a warm up, then will move down to the deep end for the middle of the class, and will come back to the shallow end to finish the class. This class provides a mixture of cardio, strength, abdominal work and stretching. Use of a noodle or water belt is recommended for exercise in the deep end.
Admission: Use your Esquimalt Recreation or Regional Recreation Pass, or pay regular drop in.

events.title: Open Court - Basketball | Jumpstart Multi Sport Court
events.description:
Check out the Jumpstart Multi Sport Court at Panorama! Free, accessible, recreational play for all ages. 

Space includes 8 basketball nets (2 adjustable), a convertible net (set to high or low height dependent on the day), picnic tables and a free play space. Sun shades are hung seasonally. Lines and nets are recreational; not regulation.

Bring or borrow equipment. Equipment may be signed out at Reception (available first come, first served).

Please limit play to 30 minutes if others are waiting. 

Code of Conduct:  Users are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

Waitlist:  If you waitlist and a space becomes available, you will be notified by email. You will be asked to accept or decline the space. Please do so in a timely manner so those subsequently waitlisted may have opportunity to fill the space if you no longer want it. 

Refund/Cancellation:  Full refund for cancellations made with more than 24 hours' notice. Cancel your session online via your account. No refund for cancellations made with less than 24 hours' notice. No-shows will be charged.

Code of Conduct:  Players are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and respectful, both on and off the court. All staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to treat others and our facilities with care and respect.

events.title: Everyone Welcome and Programs

events.title: All Levels Pickleball | Reservable
events.description:
Join us for All Levels Reservable Pickleball at Greenglade Community Centre! All skill levels welcome. Bring your own paddle and ball (a couple paddles are available to borrow from Reception). 

Check-in with Reception upon arrival. Consecutive reservations not permitted. Continued play welcome, space permitting on the day (check with Reception at the start of the next session).

******

Family drop-in rate available! Please call or email Reception for this discounted admission type. Family rate includes 2 adults and up to 3 youth (max 18 years), or 1 adult and up to 4 youth (max 18 years), living at the same address.

******

Waitlist:  If you waitlist and a space becomes available, you will be notified by email. You will be asked to accept or decline the space. Please do so in a timely manner so those subsequently waitlisted may have opportunity to fill the space if you no longer want it. 

Refund/Cancellation:  Full refund for cancellations made with more than 24 hours' notice. Cancel your session online via your account. No refund for cancellations made with less than 24 hours' notice. No-shows will be charged.

Code of Conduct:  Players are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and respectful, both on and off the court. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, positive and respectful environment where everyone is valued. All staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to treat others and our facilities with care and respect.

events.title: Open Court - Convertible Net Low | Jumpstart Multi Sport Court
events.description:
Check out the Jumpstart Multi Sport Court at Panorama! Free, accessible, recreational play for all ages. 

Space includes 8 basketball nets (2 adjustable), a convertible net (set to high or low height dependent on the day), picnic tables and a free play space. Sun shades are hung seasonally. Lines and nets are recreational; not regulation.

Bring or borrow equipment. Equipment may be signed out at Reception (available first come, first served).

Please limit play to 30 minutes if others are waiting. 

Code of Conduct:  Users are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: Deep Fit
events.description:
Moderate intensity, no impact (comfort in deep water required).
Using a belt or a noodle, this class is a great way for those with injuries or other limitations to still experience a good cardiovascular workout. This class will include strength and abdominal exercises to ensure a full body workout with no strain on bones or joints. 
Admission: Use your Esquimalt Recreation or Regional Recreation Pass, or pay regular drop in.

Combo Fit
events.description:
Moderate intensity, moderate impact (comfort in deep water recommended). This class incorporates elements from both shallow and deep fit classes. Participants will begin in the shallow end for a warm up, then will move down to the deep end for the middle of the class, and will come back to the shallow end to finish the class. This class provides a mixture of cardio, strength, abdominal work and stretching. Use of a noodle or water belt is recommended for exercise in the deep end.
Admission: Use your Esquimalt Recreation or Regional Recreation Pass, or pay regular drop in.

events.title: Skate - Stick & Puck Reserved Drop In 19yrs+ GOALIE
events.facility_name: GRP Ice Rink Green - Lesson Area 2
events.description: Enjoy practicing your goaltending in the net during a Stick and Puck session. Full gear required. Maximum 2 goalies can register. You must reserve your spot to participate. Any goalie not registered as a goalie will be asked to remain a skater. Only one net is used for goalies, the other net will remain open. Goalies must be respectful and take turns being in net. Close the arena gates behind you. You must check in with reception and the skate attendant before going on the ice to validate your reservation. Please refer to the guidelines on our website for further details.

events.title: Skate - Stick & Puck Reserved Drop In 19yrs+ SKATER
events.facility_name: GRP Ice Rink Green - Lesson Area 1
events.description: Enjoy shooting on the net without goalies. Helmet, gloves, stick and skates mandatory. You must reserve your spot to participate. There are no slap shots, scrimmages, drills or passing down the ice. Keep the front and behind the net clear at all times. Always close the arena gates behind you. You must check in with reception and the skate attendant before going on the ice to validate your reservation.

events.title: Open Court - Basketball | Jumpstart Multi Sport Court
events.description:
Check out the Jumpstart Multi Sport Court at Panorama! Free, accessible, recreational play for all ages. 

Space includes 8 basketball nets (2 adjustable), a convertible net (set to high or low height dependent on the day), picnic tables and a free play space. Sun shades are hung seasonally. Lines and nets are recreational; not regulation.

Bring or borrow equipment. Equipment may be signed out at Reception (available first come, first served).

Please limit play to 30 minutes if others are waiting. 

Code of Conduct:  Users are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: Shallow Fit
events.description:
Moderate to high intensity, moderate impact (Beginner to Advanced) 
This class features interval training, power exercises and movement through the water to challenge participants. A mixture of cardio, strength, abdominal work and stretching is provided and instructors use a variety of exercises and equipment to keep the classes new and exciting. 
Admission: Use your Esquimalt Recreation or Regional Recreation Pass, or pay regular drop in.

events.title: Skate - Stick & Puck Reserved Drop In 19yrs+ GOALIE
events.facility_name: GRP Ice Rink Green - Lesson Area 2
events.description: Enjoy practicing your goaltending in the net during a Stick and Puck session. Full gear required. Maximum 2 goalies can register. You must reserve your spot to participate. Any goalie not registered as a goalie will be asked to remain a skater. Only one net is used for goalies, the other net will remain open. Goalies must be respectful and take turns being in net. Close the arena gates behind you. You must check in with reception and the skate attendant before going on the ice to validate your reservation. Please refer to the guidelines on our website for further details.

events.title: Skate - Stick & Puck Reserved Drop In 19yrs+ SKATER
events.facility_name: GRP Ice Rink Green - Lesson Area 1
events.description: Enjoy shooting on the net without goalies. Helmet, gloves, stick and skates mandatory. You must reserve your spot to participate. There are no slap shots, scrimmages, drills or passing down the ice. Keep the front and behind the net clear at all times. Always close the arena gates behind you. You must check in with reception and the skate attendant before going on the ice to validate your reservation.

events.description: Pickleball is a racquet sport that combines elements of badminton, tennis, and table tennis. Players use solid paddles to hit a perforated polymer ball, similar to a wiffle ball, over a net. The sport shares features of other racquet sports, the dimensions and layout of a badminton court, and a net and rules similar to tennis, with a few modifications. Must be 16 or older, please bring your own paddle.

events.title: Volleyball Social Game Play | Reservable
events.description:
Join us for Volleyball Social Game Play! Registration required. 

Players of all skill levels who are comfortable with fundamental skills and basics of volleyball are welcome. Each session consists of three 25-minute games, allowing players opportunity to compete against different teams. All equipment provided.

Waitlist:  If you waitlist and a space becomes available, you will be notified by email. You will be asked to accept or decline the space. Please do so in a timely manner so those subsequently waitlisted may have opportunity to fill the space if you no longer want it. 

Refund/Cancellation:  Full refund for cancellations made with more than 24 hours' notice. Cancel your session online via your account. No refund for cancellations made with less than 24 hours' notice. No-shows will be charged.

Code of Conduct:  Players are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

GH Core and More* - Reserved Drop In
events.description: Focus on strengthening your core abdominal area, lower back and glutes. A variety of exercise equipment is used in this class suitable for everyone. This is a drop-in class no registration necessary.

events.title: Deep Fit
events.description:
Moderate intensity, no impact (comfort in deep water required).
Using a belt or a noodle, this class is a great way for those with injuries or other limitations to still experience a good cardiovascular workout. This class will include strength and abdominal exercises to ensure a full body workout with no strain on bones or joints. 
Admission: Use your Esquimalt Recreation or Regional Recreation Pass, or pay regular drop in.

events.title: All Levels Pickleball | Reservable
events.description:
Join us for All Levels Reservable Pickleball at Greenglade Community Centre! All skill levels welcome. Bring your own paddle and ball (a couple paddles available to borrow from Reception). 

Check-in with Reception upon arrival. Consecutive reservations not permitted. Continued play welcome, space permitting on the day (check with Reception at the start of the next session).

******

Family drop-in rate available! Please call or email Reception for this discounted admission type. Family rate includes 2 adults and up to 3 youth (max 18 years), or 1 adult and up to 4 youth (max 18 years), living at the same address.

******

Waitlist:  If you waitlist and a space becomes available, you will be notified by email. You will be asked to accept or decline the space. Please do so in a timely manner so those subsequently waitlisted may have opportunity to fill the space if you no longer want it. 

Refund/Cancellation:  Full refund for cancellations made with more than 24 hours' notice. Cancel your session online via your account. No refund for cancellations made with less than 24 hours' notice. No-shows will be charged.

Code of Conduct:  Players are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and respectful, both on and off the court. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, positive and respectful environment where everyone is valued. All staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to treat others and our facilities with care and respect.

events.description: Get ready for strategy games, forest laser tag adventures and endless out trips. Grab your friends or come and meet new ones while you embark on daily challenges and quests to put the pieces of the game puzzle together. This camp is the place for youth who love games of a competitive nature.  ADDITIONAL INFORMATION:PLEASE NOTE: This program has age requirements. Minimum Age: Born in 2015 – 9 Years – Going into Grade 5Maximum Age: Born in 2012 – 13 Years - Going Into Grade 8 Campers who are born 2012, entering Grade 9 and are still 13 years old have aged out of Breakout Adventure Camp.  Campers of this age are welcome to register in our Breakout Adventure Camp located at Gordon Head Youth Centre with an older age bracket. For more information, please contact maddie.parks@saanich.ca

Drop-in Program. For parents/caregivers and their children (1-5yrs.) to enjoy our toys, tricycles, cars and trucks, teeter-totters and other large motor development toys. Adults are welcome to relax and get to know other neighbourhood families and QVCC facilitators while their children play. A light snack is provided. Program takes place in the QVCC Neighbourhood Gym at 950 Kings Rd. (the entrance is at the back of the building through the parking lot).
There is a daily limit on attendees, so arrive early to avoid disappointment.  Check this calendar each morning before you come for up to the minute program cancellations. For: Parents/Caregivers and their children ages 0-5yrs Calendar: Child & Family

events.title: Balance, Strength and Stretch
events.description: This class will guide participants through a workout that focuses on balance, strength, and stretch. The class will begin with some cardio to warm up the body and prepare it for exercise. This class provides an option to participate from standing, or from a chair or wheelchair. All fitness levels for participants ages 50+ are welcome to join.

events.title: Combo Fit
events.description:
Moderate intensity, moderate impact (comfort in deep water recommended). This class incorporates elements from both shallow and deep fit classes. Participants will begin in the shallow end for a warm up, then will move down to the deep end for the middle of the class, and will come back to the shallow end to finish the class. This class provides a mixture of cardio, strength, abdominal work and stretching. Use of a noodle or water belt is recommended for exercise in the deep end.
Admission: Use your Esquimalt Recreation or Regional Recreation Pass, or pay regular drop in.

events.title: GH Pure Stretch* - Reserved Drop In

events.title: Skate - Stick & Puck Reserved Drop In 19yrs+ GOALIE
events.facility_name: GRP Ice Rink Green - Lesson Area 2
events.description: Enjoy practicing your goaltending in the net during a Stick and Puck session. Full gear required. Maximum 2 goalies can register. You must reserve your spot to participate. Any goalie not registered as a goalie will be asked to remain a skater. Only one net is used for goalies, the other net will remain open. Goalies must be respectful and take turns being in net. Close the arena gates behind you. You must check in with reception and the skate attendant before going on the ice to validate your reservation. Please refer to the guidelines on our website for further details.

events.title: Skate - Stick & Puck Reserved Drop In 19yrs+ SKATER
events.facility_name: GRP Ice Rink Green - Lesson Area 1
events.description: Enjoy shooting on the net without goalies. Helmet, gloves, stick and skates mandatory. You must reserve your spot to participate. There are no slap shots, scrimmages, drills or passing down the ice. Keep the front and behind the net clear at all times. Always close the arena gates behind you. You must check in with reception and the skate attendant before going on the ice to validate your reservation.

events.title: Open Court - Basketball | Jumpstart Multi Sport Court
events.description:
Check out the Jumpstart Multi Sport Court at Panorama! Free, accessible, recreational play for all ages. 

Space includes 8 basketball nets (2 adjustable), a convertible net (set to high or low height dependent on the day), picnic tables and a free play space. Sun shades are hung seasonally. Lines and nets are recreational; not regulation.

Bring or borrow equipment. Equipment may be signed out at Reception (available first come, first served).

Please limit play to 30 minutes if others are waiting. 

Code of Conduct:  Users are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: Advanced (4.0+) Pickleball | Reservable
events.description:
Join us for Advanced Reservable Pickleball at Greenglade Community Centre! Players of 4.0+ skill level welcome (as per Pickleball Canada 2-digit rating guide). Bring your own paddle and ball (a couple paddles are available to borrow from Reception). 

Check-in with Reception upon arrival. Consecutive reservations not permitted. Continued play welcome, space permitting on the day (check with Reception at the start of the next session).

******

Family drop-in rate available! Please call or email Reception for this discounted admission type. Family rate includes 2 adults and up to 3 youth (max 18 years), or 1 adult and up to 4 youth (max 18 years), living at the same address.

******

Waitlist:  If you waitlist and a space becomes available, you will be notified by email. You will be asked to accept or decline the space. Please do so in a timely manner so those subsequently waitlisted may have opportunity to fill the space if you no longer want it. 

Refund/Cancellation:  Full refund for cancellations made with more than 24 hours' notice. Cancel your session online via your account. No refund for cancellations made with less than 24 hours' notice. No-shows will be charged.

Code of Conduct:  Players are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and respectful, both on and off the court. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, positive and respectful environment where everyone is valued. All staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to treat others and our facilities with care and respect.

events.title: Combo Fit
events.description:
Moderate intensity, moderate impact (comfort in deep water recommended). This class incorporates elements from both shallow and deep fit classes. Participants will begin in the shallow end for a warm up, then will move down to the deep end for the middle of the class, and will come back to the shallow end to finish the class. This class provides a mixture of cardio, strength, abdominal work and stretching. Use of a noodle or water belt is recommended for exercise in the deep end.
Admission: Use your Esquimalt Recreation or Regional Recreation Pass, or pay regular drop in.

events.title: Pickleball Reserved Drop-in
events.description:
Pickleball is a racquet sport that combines elements of badminton, tennis, and table tennis. Players use solid paddles to hit a perforated polymer ball, similar to a wiffle ball, over a net. The sport shares features of other racquet sports, the dimensions and layout of a badminton court, and a net and rules similar to tennis, with a few modifications.
Players can reserve for their desired session up to 7 days in advance.

events.title: Open Court - Basketball | Jumpstart Multi Sport Court
events.description:
Check out the Jumpstart Multi Sport Court at Panorama! Free, accessible, recreational play for all ages. 

Space includes 8 basketball nets (2 adjustable), a convertible net (set to high or low height dependent on the day), picnic tables and a free play space. Sun shades are hung seasonally. Lines and nets are recreational; not regulation.

Bring or borrow equipment. Equipment may be signed out at Reception (available first come, first served).

Please limit play to 30 minutes if others are waiting. 

Code of Conduct:  Users are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: Open Court - Convertible Net High | Jumpstart Multi Sport Court
events.description:

Space includes 8 basketball nets (2 adjustable), a convertible net (set to badminton/volleyball height or pickleball height dependent on the day), picnic tables and a free play space. Sun shades are hung seasonally. 

Bring or borrow equipment. Equipment may be signed out at Reception (available first come, first served).

Please limit play to 30 minutes if others are waiting.

Code of Conduct:  Users are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. events.title: Open Court - Basketball | Jumpstart Multi Sport Court
events.description:
Check out the Jumpstart Multi Sport Court at Panorama! Free, accessible, recreational play for all ages. 

Space includes 8 basketball nets (2 adjustable), a convertible net (set to high or low height dependent on the day), picnic tables and a free play space. Sun shades are hung seasonally. Lines and nets are recreational; not regulation.

Bring or borrow equipment. Equipment may be signed out at Reception (available first come, first served).

Please limit play to 30 minutes if others are waiting. 

Code of Conduct:  Users are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and sportsmanlike. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, welcoming, positive and inclusive environment where everyone is valued and respected. Staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to be considerate, respectful of others, and take proper care for our facility and equipment.

events.title: All Levels Pickleball | Reservable
events.description:
Join us for All Levels Reservable Pickleball at Greenglade Community Centre! All skill levels welcome. Bring your own paddle and ball (a couple paddles are available to borrow from Reception). 

Check-in with Reception upon arrival. Consecutive reservations not permitted. Continued play welcome, space permitting on the day (check with Reception at the start of the next session).

******

Family drop-in rate available! Please call or email Reception for this discounted admission type. Family rate includes 2 adults and up to 3 youth (max 18 years), or 1 adult and up to 4 youth (max 18 years), living at the same address.

******

Waitlist:  If you waitlist and a space becomes available, you will be notified by email. You will be asked to accept or decline the space. Please do so in a timely manner so those subsequently waitlisted may have opportunity to fill the space if you no longer want it. 

Refund/Cancellation:  Full refund for cancellations made with more than 24 hours' notice. Cancel your session online via your account. No refund for cancellations made with less than 24 hours' notice. No-shows will be charged.

Code of Conduct:  Players are expected to be welcoming, inclusive and respectful, both on and off the court. Panorama Recreation promotes a safe, positive and respectful environment where everyone is valued. All staff, volunteers, participants and facility users are expected to treat others and our facilities with care and respect.

events.title: Skate - Stick & Puck Reserved Drop In 19yrs+ GOALIE
events.facility_name: GRP Ice Rink Green - Lesson Area 2
events.description: Enjoy practicing your goaltending in the net during a Stick and Puck session. Full gear required. Maximum 2 goalies can register. You must reserve your spot to participate. Any goalie not registered as a goalie will be asked to remain a skater. Only one net is used for goalies, the other net will remain open. Goalies must be respectful and take turns being in net. Close the arena gates behind you. You must check in with reception and the skate attendant before going on the ice to validate your reservation. Please refer to the guidelines on our website for further details.

events.title: Skate - Stick & Puck Reserved Drop In 19yrs+ SKATER
events.facility_name: GRP Ice Rink Green - Lesson Area 1
events.description: Enjoy shooting on the net without goalies. Helmet, gloves, stick and skates mandatory. You must reserve your spot to participate. There are no slap shots, scrimmages, drills or passing down the ice. Keep the front and behind the net clear at all times. Always close the arena gates behind you. You must check in with reception and the skate attendant before going on the ice to validate your reservation.

events.description:
Moderate intensity, no impact (comfort in deep water required).

Admission: Use your Esquimalt Recreation or Regional Recreation Pass, or pay regular drop in.

events.title: Combo Fit
events.description:
Moderate intensity, moderate impact (comfort in deep water recommended). This class incorporates elements from both shallow and deep fit classes. Participants will begin in the shallow end for a warm up, then will move down to the deep end for the middle of the class, and will come back to the shallow end to finish the class. This class provides a mixture of cardio, strength, abdominal work and stretching. Use of a noodle or water belt is recommended for exercise in the deep end.
Admission: Use your Esquimalt Recreation or Regional Recreation Pass, or pay regular drop in.

Pickleball Reserved Drop-in
events.description:
Pickleball is a racquet sport that combines elements of badminton, tennis, and table tennis. Players use solid paddles to hit a perforated polymer ball, similar to a wiffle ball, over a net. The sport shares features of other racquet sports, the dimensions and layout of a badminton court, and a net and rules similar to tennis, with a few modifications.
Players can reserve for their desired session up to 7 days in advance.

events.title: PKS Triple Fit ** Reserved Drop In
events.description: This activity is rated as moderate intensity. Please bring your own water bottle and gym towel. Equipment will be provided. No class on statutory holidays. Please visit our website to access the fitness class schedule.

events.title: Pickleball - Reserved Drop In
events.description: This is a Reserved Drop In Pickleball session at SCP. Players can reserve your spot up to 7 days in advance starting at 7:30 am.

events.title: Youth Drop-in
events.description: For youth 10-18 come and join us for a variety of activities or just hang out in an inclusive space! Dinner is served at 5pm. Calendar: Youth Programs

events.title: Skate - Stick & Puck Reserved Drop In 19yrs+ GOALIE
events.facility_name: GRP Ice Rink Green - Lesson Area 2
events.description: Enjoy practicing your goaltending in the net during a Stick and Puck session. Full gear required. Maximum 2 goalies can register. You must reserve your spot to participate. Any goalie not registered as a goalie will be asked to remain a skater. Only one net is used for goalies, the other net will remain open. Goalies must be respectful and take turns being in net. Close the arena gates behind you. You must check in with reception and the skate attendant before going on the ice to validate your reservation. Please refer to the guidelines on our website for further details.

events.title: Skate - Stick & Puck Reserved Drop In 19yrs+ SKATER
events.facility_name: GRP Ice Rink Green - Lesson Area 1
events.description: Enjoy shooting on the net without goalies. Helmet, gloves, stick and skates mandatory. You must reserve your spot to participate. There are no slap shots, scrimmages, drills or passing down the ice. Keep the front and behind the net clear at all times. Always close the arena gates behind you. You must check in with reception and the skate attendant before going on the ice to validate your reservation.

events.title: GH Basic Yoga* - Reserved Drop In
events.description: A basic beginner-friendly class that introduces key poses and breathing techniques at a slower pace. Perfect for those new to yoga or looking to refresh their practice.

events.title: Cardio Blast
events.description: A more challenging, shallow water class that focuses on high energy cardio moves and strength work.

events.title: SCP Partyfit®** - Reserved Drop In
events.description: The ultimate high-intensity, high energy, dance fitness workout designed to increase participants fitness levels, mixing strength, cardio, balance and core exercises with fun and simple dance moves. No prior dance experience required and all fitness levels welcome.

events.title: Basketball - Reserved Drop In
events.description: This is a Registered Drop In Basketball program at SCP. Players can reserve a spot up to 7 days in advance starting at 7:30 am.

events.facility_name: 950 Kings Road, (follow the parking lot around the back of the yellow building to find the entrance)
events.description:
Drop-in Program. For parents/caregivers and their children (1-5yrs.) to enjoy our toys, tricycles, cars and trucks, teeter-totters and other large motor development toys. Adults are welcome to relax and get to know other neighbourhood families and QVCC facilitators while their children play. A light snack is provided. Program takes place in the QVCC Neighbourhood Gym at 950 Kings Rd. (the entrance is at the back of the building through the parking lot).
There is a daily limit on attendees, so arrive early to avoid disappointment.  Check this calendar each morning before you come for up to the minute program cancellations. For: Parents/Caregivers and their children ages 0-5yrs Calendar: Child & Family

events.facility_name: 950 Kings Road, (follow the parking lot around the back of the yellow building to find the entrance)
events.description:
Drop-in Program. For parents/caregivers and their children (1-5yrs.) to enjoy our toys, tricycles, cars and trucks, teeter-totters and other large motor development toys. Adults are welcome to relax and get to know other neighbourhood families and QVCC facilitators while their children play. A light snack is provided. Program takes place in the QVCC Neighbourhood Gym at 950 Kings Rd. (the entrance is at the back of the building through the parking lot).
There is a daily limit on attendees, so arrive early to avoid disappointment.  Check this calendar each morning before you come for up to the minute program cancellations. For: Parents/Caregivers and their children ages 0-5yrs Calendar: Child & Family

events.title: Drop in Kindergym
events.facility_name: 950 Kings Road, (follow the parking lot around the back of the yellow building to find the entrance)
events.description:
Drop-in Program. For parents/caregivers and their children (1-5yrs.) to enjoy our toys, tricycles, cars and trucks, teeter-totters and other large motor development toys. Adults are welcome to relax and get to know other neighbourhood families and QVCC facilitators while their children play. A light snack is provided. Program takes place in the QVCC Neighbourhood Gym at 950 Kings Rd. (the entrance is at the back of the building through the parking lot).
There is a daily limit on attendees, so arrive early to avoid disappointment.  Check this calendar each morning before you come for up to the minute program cancellations. For: Parents/Caregivers and their children ages 0-5yrs Calendar: Child & Family

Drop-in Program. For parents/caregivers and their children (1-5yrs.) to enjoy our toys, tricycles, cars and trucks, teeter-totters and other large motor development toys. Adults are welcome to relax and get to know other neighbourhood families and QVCC facilitators while their children play. Snack provided. Program takes place in the QVCC Neighbourhood Gym at 950 Kings Rd. (the entrance is at the back ofthe building through the parking lot).
There is a daily limit on attendees, so arrive early to avoid disappointment.  Check this calendar each morning before you come for up to the minute program cancellations. Calendar: Child & Family

events.title: Bootcamp Drop in Wed 9:30am Summer 2 (Oaklands Park)
Drop in Kindergym
events.description:
Drop-in Program. For parents/caregivers and their children (1-5yrs.) to enjoy our toys, tricycles, cars and trucks, teeter-totters and other large motor development toys. Adults are welcome to relax and get to know other neighbourhood families and QVCC facilitators while their children play. Snack provided. Program takes place in the QVCC Neighbourhood Gym at 950 Kings Rd. (the entrance is at the back ofthe building through the parking lot).
There is a daily limit on attendees, so arrive early to avoid disappointment.  Check this calendar each morning before you come for up to the minute program cancellations. Calendar: Child & Family

events.title: Lanes & Leisure - All Ages
events.description: Lanes (by pace), water walking, leisure pool and hot areas are available. Lane configuration may vary.     Space may be shared with lessons or advanced aquatic programs.    CHILD ADMISSION POLICY:  Children under the age of 7 must be within arm’s reach of a responsible person 16 years or older at all times, including in changerooms, on the pool deck, and in the pool. A maximum of 3 children under 7 may be supervised by a responsible person. Spaces: 80

events.title: Weightroom Attendant on Duty
events.description: The weightroom is a comprehensive workout facility for individuals to meet their exercise needs.  It is fully accessible with automatic doors and wide aisles.   The weightroom is restricted to ages 15 years and older. Youth ages 13-15 years must complete a Youth Weight Room Orientation and upon completion, may attend on their own. Spaces: 75

events.title: Limited Lane & Leisure (All Ages)
events.description: Limited capcity (1-2 single lanes) are available by pace. Lane configuration may vary. Steam room, sauna, and hot tub are open. Leisure pool available for families with children of all ages/    CHILD ADMISSION POLICY:  Children under the age of 7 must be within arm’s reach of a responsible person 16 years or older at all times, including in changerooms, on the pool deck, and in the pool. A maximum of 3 children under 7 may be supervised by a responsible person.   Children 7-9 years require an adult in the facility durning their visit. Spaces: 80

The weightroom is restricted to ages 15 years and older. Youth ages 13-15 years must complete a Youth Weight Room Orientation and upon completion, may attend on their own. Spaces: 75

events.title: Total Body Conditionin
events.description: Oak Bay Group Fitness Drop-In PDF schedule. Registration opens up to four days in advance via Oak Bay PerfectMind.

events.title: Reduced Lanes & Leisure
events.description: Reduced capacity (3-6 single lanes) are available by pace, including water walking. Lane configuration may vary. Steam room, sauna, and hot tub are open. Leisure pool available for families with children of all ages/    CHILD ADMISSION POLICY:  Children under the age of 7 must be within arm’s reach of a responsible person 16 years or older at all times, including in changerooms, on the pool deck, and in the pool. A maximum of 3 children under 7 may be supervised by a responsible person.   Children 7-9 years require an adult in the facility durning their visit. Spaces: 80
